zh.lifeSummary: Sarah Foster於大約1384年出生在英國史洛普爾艾頓 白 比曉普斯 卡素。她於大約1404年與Thomas Brosyard Eyton在英國英格蘭史洛普爾艾頓 亞爾 維姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國。

nameMeanings.name: Foster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Forster   ‘worker in a forest’.
English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English foster ‘foster parent’ (Old English fōstre, a derivative of fōstrian ‘to nourish or rear’). But other explanations are equally or more likely.
English: from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors’; see Forster 2.

variants.name: Fewster
meanings: English (mainly East Yorkshire and Durham): occupational name from Anglo-Norman French and Middle English fu(y)ster ‘maker of saddletrees’. See also Foster .

variants.name: Forster
meanings: English: shortened form of   Forrester  , an occupational name for someone who worked in a forest.
English: possibly a Norman French nickname or occupational name from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors or shears, cutler’. Compare Force and Forse .

German (mainly Förster): agent derivative of Middle High German forst ‘forest’, denoting someone who lived in or near a royal forest, or who worked in one (see Forst ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Foerster .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Sarah Foster was born about 1384, in Eyton by Bishops Castle, Shropshire, England. She married Thomas Brosyard Eyton about 1404, in Eyton near Wem, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Fosterは約1384年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアエイトン ビー ビショプス カストルで生まれた。約1404年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアエイトン ネアル ウエムでThomas Brosyard Eytonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Foster(은)는 대략 1384년 영국 슈럽실르 이턴 비 비숍스 카스틀에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1404년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 이턴 니아어 w에서 Thomas Brosyard Eyton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sarah Foster nasceu aproximadamente 1384, em Eyton by Bishops Castle, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Thomas Brosyard Eyton aproximadamente 1404, em Eyton near Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Sarah Foster родилась около 1384 г. в местности эитон би бишопс кастл, шропшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Brosyard Eyton около 1404 г. в местности эитон нир вем, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности Англия.
